idea-plugin.name: Remote ADB Connector
idea-plugin.description:
Remote ADB Connector

Connect to Android devices attached to a remote Windows ADB server from your Linux Android Studio instance — with a single click.

Features

  • One-click connection — enter the Windows IP, click Connect, done.
  • Automatic device discovery — detects USB devices on the remote server.
  • TCP mode switching — automatically runs adb tcpip on the remote device.
  • Continuous monitoring — watches for disconnections and auto-reconnects.
  • No shell scripts — everything is implemented natively in Kotlin.
  • Per-process ADB server switching — never modifies your global environment.

How it works

The plugin connects to the ADB server running on your Windows machine, discovers USB-connected Android devices, switches them to TCP mode, and connects them to your local Linux ADB server — all automatically.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.5

  • Added selective per-device connection options (WiFi and USB) in the Available Devices list
  • Implemented dynamic port forwarding for USB mode to support multiple concurrent USB connections without conflicts
  • Enabled continuous Windows server background monitoring alongside local Linux connection tracking

1.0.3

  • Added interactive "Help" button in the tool window showing setup commands for exposing the Windows ADB server
  • Updated documentation with network setup guides and visual mockups

1.0.2

  • Added light and dark themed plugin icons for Marketplace and IDE Plugin Manager

1.0.1

  • Initial release
  • Full connection workflow: discover, prepare, connect, monitor
  • Auto-reconnect on device disconnection
  • Persistent settings
  • Tool window with real-time status
  • Custom branding and icons for sidebar and plugin marketplace
